ansible.snmp.get.short_description: Perform an SNMP get against a remote device for one or more OIDs
ansible.snmp.get.description: Perform an SNMP get against a remote device for one or more OIDs

best_guess.description: This setting controls how return value oids are parsed. Setting to 0 causes a regular lookup. Setting to 1 causes a regular expression match (defined as -Ib in snmpcmd). Setting to 2 causes a random access lookup (defined as -IR in snmpcmd).

enums.description: Set to `True` to have integer return values converted to enumeration identifiers if possible.

long_names.description: Set to `True` to have OIDS generated preferring longer Mib name convention.

oids.description: A dictionary of entries to get from the remote device

numeric.description: Set to `True to have `oids` returned untranslated (i.e. dotted-decimal).

sprint_value.description: Set to `True` to have return values formatted with netsnmp's sprint_value function. This will result in certain data types being returned in non-canonical format. Values returned with this option set may not be appropriate for 'set' operations

ansible.snmp.set.short_description: Perform an SNMP set against a remote device for one or more OIDs
ansible.snmp.set.description: Perform an SNMP get against a remote device for one or more OIDs

oids.description: A dictionary of OID, value to set on the remote device

ansible.snmp.walk.short_description: Perform an SNMP walk against a remote device for one or more OIDs
ansible.snmp.walk.description: Perform an SNMP walk against a remote device for one or more OIDs

best_guess.description: This setting controls how return value oids are parsed. Setting to 0 causes a regular lookup. Setting to 1 causes a regular expression match (defined as -Ib in snmpcmd). Setting to 2 causes a random access lookup (defined as -IR in snmpcmd).

enums.description: Set to `True` to have integer return values converted to enumeration identifiers if possible.

long_names.description: Set to `True` to have OIDS generated preferring longer Mib name convention.

oids.description: A dictionary of entries to walk on the remote device

numeric.description: Set to `True to have `oids` returned untranslated (i.e. dotted-decimal).

sprint_value.description: Set to `True` to have return values formatted with netsnmp's sprint_value function. This will result in certain data types being returned in non-canonical format. Values returned with this option set may not be appropriate for 'set' operations.
